tagged_story_for_tts: <[silence:3400ms]>Bent metal stays bent. <[silence:1300ms]> Shape memory alloys forget nothing.<[silence:1300ms]>"Metal’s memory" refers to alloys like nitinol—a mix of nickel and titanium—that "remember" their original shape after being twisted or crushed.<[silence:700ms]>Like a crumpled paper springing back when heated, but with metal.<[silence:1300ms]>Here’s how it works:<[silence:700ms]>At low temps, the alloy’s atoms lock into a deformed pattern—bend a nitinol wire, and it stays bent.<[silence:700ms]>Add heat (even body warmth), and the atoms snap back to their trained structure, pulling the metal with them.<[silence:1300ms]>Example:<[silence:700ms]>A nitinol stent, compressed into a tube, expands inside an artery when blood flow warms it—no surgery needed.<[silence:700ms]>NASA uses these alloys for satellite antennas that unfold in space, triggered by sunlight.<[silence:1300ms]>Precision matters:<[silence:700ms]>A 1mm nitinol wire can lift 5kg when activated—stronger than human muscle per gram.<[silence:2000ms]>Three related subjects are adaptive materials, thermal triggers, and self-healing metals.<[silence:1200ms]>
clean_story: Bent metal stays bent.  Shape memory alloys forget nothing. "Metal’s memory" refers to alloys like nitinol—a mix of nickel and titanium—that "remember" their original shape after being twisted or crushed. Like a crumpled paper springing back when heated, but with metal. Here’s how it works:At low temps, the alloy’s atoms lock into a deformed pattern—bend a nitinol wire, and it stays bent. Add heat (even body warmth), and the atoms snap back to their trained structure, pulling the metal with them. Example:A nitinol stent, compressed into a tube, expands inside an artery when blood flow warms it—no surgery needed. NASA uses these alloys for satellite antennas that unfold in space, triggered by sunlight. Precision matters:A 1mm nitinol wire can lift 5kg when activated—stronger than human muscle per gram. Three related subjects are adaptive materials, thermal triggers, and self-healing metals.
